tables.name: mv_scheme_account_mapping
tables.description: The table contains mappings between accounting schemes and various accounts used in financial operations. It includes details about account identifiers, account numbers, and associated parameters for different categories such as banks, suppliers, and customers. This data can be utilized for financial reporting, account management, and analyzing accounting practices within different schemes.

columns.name: sp_folder_id
columns.description: Identifier representing the specific folder under which the financial accounts are organized.

columns.name: sp_accounting_scheme_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the accounting scheme associated with the account mapping.

columns.name: sp_account_id
columns.description: Identifier for the specific account being used in the financial operations.

columns.name: geo_calc_direction
columns.description: Direction of calculation indicating the flow of transactions, which may affect how accounting entries are recorded.

columns.name: scheme_nb_accounting_entry
columns.description: A count of the total accounting entries associated with a specific accounting scheme.

columns.name: account_number
columns.description: The number assigned to the account, used for unique identification in financial transactions.

columns.name: account_label
columns.description: A descriptive label for the account, providing clarity about the account's purpose or type.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_bank
columns.description: Parameter representing the bank account identifier used in the balance sheet.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_bank_petty_cash
columns.description: Parameter indicating the petty cash account identifier for banking entries on the balance sheet.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_suppliers
columns.description: Identifier for the accounts related to suppliers as recorded in the balance sheet.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_customers
columns.description: Identifier for the accounts related to customers as recorded in the balance sheet.

columns.name: param_acc_pl_expense
columns.description: Parameter representing the account identifier used for expense entries in the profit and loss statement.

columns.name: param_acc_pl_expense_financial_costs
columns.description: Specifies the account identifier for financial costs recorded in the profit and loss statement.

columns.name: param_acc_pl_income
columns.description: Identifier for accounts related to income as recorded in the profit and loss statement.

columns.name: param_acc_pl_income_financial_income
columns.description: Specifies the account identifier for financial income in the profit and loss statement.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_vat_deductible
columns.description: Parameter for the account identifier used to track deductible VAT in the balance sheet.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_vat_collected
columns.description: Identifier for the account that tracks VAT collected, as reflected in the balance sheet.

tables.name: mv_accounting_entry_enriched
tables.description: The table contains enriched accounting entries, designed to provide detailed insights into financial transactions. This includes data on debits, credits, and balances, as well as various related accounting parameters. Colleagues may find it useful for auditing, financial reporting, and analyzing financial performance across different accounts and document types.

columns.name: sp_folder_id
columns.description: Identifies the folder associated with the accounting entry, helping organize and categorize financial transactions within the system.

columns.name: sp_accounting_entry_id
columns.description: Represents the unique identifier for each accounting entry, facilitating tracking and referencing specific transactions.

columns.name: sp_accounting_scheme_id
columns.description: Denotes the accounting scheme applicable to the entry, which defines the framework for recording financial transactions.

columns.name: sp_document_type_id
columns.description: Specifies the type of document related to the accounting entry, aiding in the classification of transactions.

columns.name: accounting_entry_number
columns.description: Serves as a sequential number assigned to each accounting entry for easy identification and retrieval.

columns.name: total_debit
columns.description: Summarizes the total amount of debits recorded in the accounting entry, reflecting outflows or expenses.

columns.name: total_credit
columns.description: Summarizes the total amount of credits recorded in the accounting entry, representing inflows or revenues.

columns.name: total_balance
columns.description: Calculates the net balance resulting from the total debits and credits, providing a snapshot of the account's standing.

columns.name: nb_lines
columns.description: Indicates the number of line items included in the accounting entry, showcasing the level of detail in the transaction.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_pl_expense_amount
columns.description: Represents the calculated amount for expenses within the profit and loss accounting context, aiding in financial reporting.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_pl_expense_purchase_amount
columns.description: Indicates the calculated amount specifically for purchase-related expenses in the profit and loss accounting context.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_pl_income_revenue_amount
columns.description: Reflects the calculated amount for income or revenue within the profit and loss accounting framework.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_bank_amount
columns.description: Summarizes the calculated balance related to bank accounts, essential for balance sheet reporting.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_suspense_amount
columns.description: Indicates the calculated amount associated with suspense accounts, used for temporarily holding transactions that require clarification.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_deferred_expense_amount
columns.description: Represents the calculated amount for deferred expenses on the balance sheet, which are costs incurred but not yet recognized.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_deferred_income_amount
columns.description: Shows the calculated amount for deferred income, indicating revenue received but not yet earned from a balance sheet perspective.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_accrued_expense_amount
columns.description: Summarizes the calculated amount of accrued expenses, which are costs that have been incurred but not yet paid.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_provision_liabilities_amount
columns.description: Indicates the calculated amount for provisions or liabilities, reflecting anticipated obligations on the balance sheet.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_suppliers_accrued_invoices_amount
columns.description: Highlights the calculated amount of accrued invoices related to suppliers, essential for accurate liability reporting.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_customers_invoices_to_be_issued_amount
columns.description: Represents the calculated amount for invoices that are to be issued to customers, indicating future revenue adjustments.

columns.name: geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate
columns.description: Shows the calculated rate of VAT that is deductible for the accounting entry, useful for tax reporting and compliance.

columns.name: geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate
columns.description: Indicates the calculated rate of VAT that has been collected from sales, crucial for understanding tax liabilities.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_suppliers_amount
columns.description: Represents the calculated total amount owed to suppliers, aiding in accounts payable management.

columns.name: geo_calc_acc_bs_customers_amount
columns.description: Summarizes the calculated total amount owed by customers, essential for managing accounts receivable.

columns.name: document_type_code
columns.description: Contains the code representing the specific type of document related to the accounting entry, useful for categorization.

columns.name: document_type_label
columns.description: Provides the label description for the document type associated with the accounting entry, facilitating easier understanding.

columns.name: param_dt_opening_balance
columns.description: Indicates the parameter for the opening balance, essential for establishing starting points in accounting periods.

columns.name: param_dt_general_journal
columns.description: Represents the parameter setting for the general journal, which is key to maintaining a comprehensive record of transactions.

columns.name: param_acc_pl_expense
columns.description: Specifies the parameter for recording expenses in the profit and loss context, critical for accurate financial reporting.

columns.name: param_acc_pl_expense_purchase
columns.description: Denotes the parameter for expenses specifically related to purchases, aiding in detailed expense tracking.

columns.name: param_acc_pl_income
columns.description: Indicates the parameter for revenue recognition in the profit and loss accounting framework, important for financial analysis.

columns.name: param_acc_pl_income_revenue
columns.description: Specifies the parameter for reporting income and revenue, essential for understanding business performance.

columns.name: scheme_param_acc_bs_suppliers
columns.description: Represents the parameter for the accounting scheme related to suppliers, necessary for managing supplier accounts.

columns.name: scheme_param_acc_bs_customers
columns.description: Denotes the parameter for the accounting scheme associated with customers, key for managing customer accounts.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_vat_deductible
columns.description: Indicates the parameter for VAT that is deductible on the balance sheet, important for tax compliance.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_vat_collected
columns.description: Specifies the parameter for VAT that has been collected, crucial for understanding tax obligations.

columns.name: param_acc_bs_partners_current_accounts
columns.description: Represents the parameter for managing current accounts related to partners, aiding in partnership accounting.

columns.name: scheme_nb_accounting_entry
columns.description: Summarizes the total number of accounting entries within the scheme, providing insights into transaction volume.

columns.name: geo_accounting_scheme_type_code
columns.description: Contains the type code for the accounting scheme, helping to categorize and differentiate between various accounting methods.

tables.name: mv_abnormal_balance_detection
tables.description: The table focuses on detecting abnormal account balances. It includes information about accounts, amounts, and associated dates. This data can be used to identify potential discrepancies in account balances, analyze account behavior, and monitor the performance of supplier and customer accounts.

columns.name: sp_folder_id
columns.description: Represents the unique identifier for the specific folder associated with the account data, helping to categorize and manage account entries efficiently.

columns.name: sp_data_gl_id
columns.description: The unique identifier for the General Ledger entry related to the account, facilitating tracking and reconciliation with financial records.

columns.name: sp_account_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the account being monitored, essential for distinguishing between different account records.

columns.name: amount
columns.description: Specifies the monetary value associated with the account entry, which is crucial for evaluating financial activity and discrepancies.

columns.name: account_number
columns.description: The primary number assigned to the account, used for identification and reference within financial systems.

columns.name: account_label
columns.description: A descriptive label for the account that provides additional context for its purpose or nature, aiding in clarity and understanding.

columns.name: account_class
columns.description: Indicates the classification of the account, which helps in segmenting accounts based on their characteristics or usage.

columns.name: aux_account_number
columns.description: An auxiliary account number that may provide additional identification or categorization for related accounts.

columns.name: aux_account_label
columns.description: An auxiliary label for the account that offers further context and details which may not be captured in the main account label.

columns.name: accounting_entry_date
columns.description: The date when the financial entry was recorded, important for tracking the timing of transactions and understanding cash flow.

columns.name: clearing_date
columns.description: The date when the account balance was officially cleared, helping to ascertain the timing of reconciliations and transactions.

columns.name: account_balance
columns.description: Reflects the current balance of the account, which is pivotal for assessing the financial health and activity of the account.

columns.name: entry_count
columns.description: The total number of entries associated with the account, providing insight into the frequency and volume of transactions.

columns.name: expected_balance_type
columns.description: Defines the anticipated classification of the balance, which aids in comparing expected versus actual account performance.

columns.name: actual_balance_type
columns.description: Indicates the classification of the current balance, playing a crucial role in discrepancy analysis and account review.

columns.name: is_abnormal_balance
columns.description: A flag indicating whether the account balance is considered abnormal, which is key for identifying irregularities that may require further investigation.

columns.name: is_supplier_account
columns.description: Denotes whether the account is classified as a supplier account, providing context for the relationship and transactions involved.

columns.name: is_customer_account
columns.description: Indicates if the account is a customer account, helping in distinguishing account types for analytical purposes.

tables.name: mv_gl_by_document_type
tables.description: The table contains data related to general ledger entries categorized by document type. It records essential details such as account IDs, transaction amounts, and dates. This information can be utilized for financial analysis, ensuring accurate accounting practices, and tracking changes over time, among other accounting-related queries.

columns.name: sp_folder_id
columns.description: Identifier for the folder associated with the specific document, used for organizational purposes within the ledger system.

columns.name: sp_data_gl_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the general ledger data entry, allowing for precise tracking of financial records.

columns.name: sp_accounting_entry_id
columns.description: Identifier for each individual accounting entry, facilitating easy reference and audit trails.

columns.name: sp_account_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the specific account involved in the transaction, essential for accurate financial reporting.

columns.name: sp_third_party_id
columns.description: Identifier for third-party entities associated with the transaction, which may include vendors or customers.

columns.name: sp_data_user_id
columns.description: Identifier for the user who entered the data, providing accountability and traceability for the entries.

columns.name: amount
columns.description: The monetary value associated with the transaction, representing the difference in the ledger for this entry.

columns.name: debit
columns.description: Total debit amount recorded in the transaction, contributing to the fundamental accounting principles of double-entry bookkeeping.

columns.name: credit
columns.description: Total credit amount recorded in the transaction, balancing the corresponding debits in accordance with accounting standards.

columns.name: accounting_entry_date
columns.description: The date on which the accounting entry was recorded, critical for tracking financial events accurately.

columns.name: valid_date
columns.description: The date indicating when the entry is valid for accounting purposes, influencing reporting and compliance.

columns.name: doc_date
columns.description: The date when the document or transaction occurred, important for understanding the timeline of financial activities.

columns.name: doc_ref
columns.description: Reference number for the document related to the entry, aiding in easy retrieval and verification of supporting documentation.

columns.name: creation_date
columns.description: The date the entry was created in the ledger system, useful for understanding the timeliness of data input.

columns.name: clearing_ref
columns.description: Reference number for any clearing transactions related to this entry, significant for reconciling offsets.

columns.name: clearing_date
columns.description: The date when any clearing transactions occurred, which is important for closing and finalizing financial records.

columns.name: accounting_entry_label
columns.description: Descriptive label providing information about the nature or purpose of the accounting entry for clarity and context.

columns.name: geo_calc_direction
columns.description: Indicates the directional aspect of the calculation related to the geographic allocation, relevant for spatial financial analysis.

columns.name: data_user_code
columns.description: Code representing the user who inputted the data, critical for performance tracking and error accountability.

columns.name: document_type_id
columns.description: Identifier for the type of document, categorizing the entry for reporting and analysis purposes.

columns.name: document_type_code
columns.description: Code representing the type of document, helping in quick identification and classification in the ledger.

columns.name: document_type_label
columns.description: Label describing the document type, providing intuitive insights into the category of the financial entry.

columns.name: param_dt_opening_balance
columns.description: Parameter indicating the opening balance at a specific date, essential for examining initial financial standing.

columns.name: param_dt_general_journal
columns.description: Parameter detailing the general journal entries, which are foundational to financial record-keeping.

columns.name: dt_total_balance
columns.description: The cumulative balance after all entries have been processed, representing the financial status at a given moment.

columns.name: dt_nb_accounting_entry
columns.description: Total number of accounting entries recorded, providing insights into the volume of transactions processed.

columns.name: geo_calc_nb_entry_batch_by_doc_type
columns.description: Counts the number of entries in batches by document type, aiding in analysis of transaction patterns.

columns.name: geo_calc_nb_entry_manual_by_doc_type
columns.description: Counts the number of manually entered entries by document type, important for understanding manual intervention in data accuracy.

tables.name: mv_vendor_master
tables.description: The table contains information about vendors, including their identification details and banking information. This data can be used for vendor management purposes, such as onboarding vendors, verifying their credentials, and facilitating payments. Key information includes vendor IDs, registration numbers, and bank account details.

columns.name: sp_folder_id
columns.description: Identifier for the specific folder associated with the vendor, used for organization and categorization within the system.

columns.name: sp_vendor_id
columns.description: Unique identifier assigned to each vendor for tracking and reference throughout the vendor management process.

columns.name: aux_account_number
columns.description: Alternative account number for the vendor, which may be used for various accounting purposes or additional banking information.

columns.name: aux_account_label
columns.description: Descriptive label associated with the alternative account number to clarify its purpose or usage.

columns.name: vat_registration_number
columns.description: Unique number assigned to the vendor for VAT purposes, important for tax compliance and invoicing.

columns.name: company_registration_number
columns.description: Official registration number of the vendor's company, required for legal identification and verification processes.

columns.name: country
columns.description: Country where the vendor is located, which is crucial for regional compliance and logistics.

columns.name: city
columns.description: City in which the vendor operates or is registered, providing additional geographical context.

columns.name: postal_code
columns.description: Postal code associated with the vendor's address, necessary for accurate mail delivery and verification.

columns.name: address
columns.description: Complete address of the vendor, including street details, which is important for communications and shipping.

columns.name: bank_iban
columns.description: International Bank Account Number assigned to the vendor's bank account, used for international transactions.

columns.name: bank_swift
columns.description: SWIFT code representing the vendor's bank, essential for identifying the bank during international money transfers.

columns.name: bank_account_number
columns.description: Specific bank account number of the vendor, required for processing payments and transactions.

columns.name: bank_key
columns.description: Bank key associated with the vendor's account, which may be necessary for payment processing and accounting.

columns.name: bank_country
columns.description: Country where the vendor's bank is located, important for understanding the banking regulations that apply.

columns.name: bank_control_key
columns.description: Control key used for bank account verification purposes, providing an additional layer of security in transactions.

tables.name: mv_potential_duplicates_supplier
tables.description: The table lists potential duplicate suppliers within the system. It includes data such as identifiers, amounts associated with transactions, and the date of documentation. Possible use cases include identifying and merging duplicate supplier accounts, tracking the frequency of occurrences, and analyzing financial data associated with these suppliers.

columns.name: sp_folder_id
columns.description: Identifies the specific folder associated with the supplier, providing context for where the supplier's records are stored within the system.

columns.name: sp_third_party_id
columns.description: Represents the unique identifier assigned to the third-party supplier, facilitating the link between the supplier and external systems.

columns.name: amount
columns.description: Indicates the financial amount related to transactions involving the supplier, which can be critical for auditing and reconciling accounts.

columns.name: doc_date
columns.description: Represents the date when the documentation for the supplier was created or recorded, essential for tracking historical changes and activities.

columns.name: doc_ref
columns.description: Contains the reference number or code for the documentation related to the supplier, serving as a pointer for further details or verification.

columns.name: occurrence_count
columns.description: Shows the number of times a potential duplicate supplier has been detected, useful for identifying heavily duplicated accounts.

columns.name: gl_ids
columns.description: Lists the general ledger IDs associated with the supplier, allowing tracking of related financial transactions across different accounts.

columns.name: entry_ids
columns.description: Contains the entry IDs linked to transactions of the supplier, providing a way to trace back individual transactions for further analysis.

Possible use cases include financial reporting, analyzing account balances, and auditing transactions to ensure accuracy and compliance.

functions.name: fn_customer_typology
functions.definition:
WITH
params AS (
  SELECT
    CAST(p_start_date AS DATE) AS start_date,
    CAST(p_end_date AS DATE) AS end_date,
    CAST(last_days AS INT) AS last_days_int
),
flagged AS (
  SELECT
    aux_account_number,
    aux_account_label,
    sp_folder_id,
    sp_accounting_entry_id,
    amount,
    accounting_entry_date,
    CASE
      WHEN accounting_entry_date >= date_add(DAY, -params.last_days_int, params.end_date) THEN ABS(amount)
      ELSE CAST(0 AS DECIMAL(30,2))
    END AS recent_amount
  FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_gl_core
  CROSS JOIN params
  WHERE sp_folder_id = p_sp_folder_id
    AND param_acc_bs_customers = 1
    AND aux_account_number IS NOT NULL
    AND accounting_entry_date BETWEEN params.start_date AND params.end_date
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    aux_account_number,
    aux_account_label,
    sp_folder_id,
    COUNT(*) AS nb_entries,
    COUNT(DISTINCT sp_accounting_entry_id) AS nb_accounting_entries,
    SUM(ABS(amount)) AS volume,
    SUM(recent_amount) AS recent_volume,
    MAX(accounting_entry_date) AS last_activity_date,
    SUM(amount) AS balance
  FROM flagged
  GROUP BY aux_account_number, aux_account_label, sp_folder_id
)
SELECT
  aux_account_number,
  aux_account_label,
  aggregated.sp_folder_id,
  nb_entries,
  nb_accounting_entries,
  volume,
  recent_volume,
  months_between(params.end_date, last_activity_date) AS last_activity_months,
  balance,
  SUM(volume) OVER () AS type_total_volume,
  last_activity_date
FROM aggregated
CROSS JOIN params
functions.description: For each customer account, returns: number of entries, number of accounting entries, total transaction volume, recent transaction volume (last N days), months since last activity, last activity date, account balance, and total volume for the account type. Used to identify inactive customers, customers with open balances, and concentration risks. One row per customer account.

functions.name: fn_vat_anomaly_detection
functions.definition:
WITH
  -- CTE 1: Résolution dossier → geo_file_version_id
  folder_info AS (
    SELECT f.id AS sp_folder_id, f.geo_file_version_id
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_folder f
    WHERE f.id = folder_id
  ),

  -- CTE 2: Taux fournis par l'utilisateur (si non vide)
  user_provided_rates AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT CAST(TRIM(rate_str) AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS legal_rate
    FROM (SELECT EXPLODE(SPLIT(legal_vat_rates, ',')) AS rate_str)
    WHERE legal_vat_rates IS NOT NULL
      AND TRIM(legal_vat_rates) != ''
  ),

  -- CTE 3: Taux légaux du référentiel pays (fallback)
  reference_rates AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT CAST(ref.param_default_value AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS legal_rate
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_geo_parameter_default_values_by_country ref
    INNER JOIN folder_info fi ON ref.geo_file_version_id = fi.geo_file_version_id
    WHERE ref.geo_parameter_type_code = 'param_general_vat_rates'
      AND (ref.country_key IS NULL OR ref.country_key = country_key)
  ),

  -- CTE 4: Priorité user > référentiel
  legal_vat_rates AS (
    SELECT legal_rate FROM user_provided_rates
    WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_provided_rates) > 0
    UNION ALL
    SELECT legal_rate FROM reference_rates
    WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_provided_rates) = 0
  ),

  -- CTE 5: Fréquence TVA déductible (achats)
  deductible_rate_freq AS (
    SELECT
      ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2) AS vat_rate,
      COUNT(*) AS cnt,
      ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) AS pct_of_total
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_accounting_entry_enriched e
    WHERE e.sp_folder_id = folder_id
      AND e.param_acc_pl_expense_purchase = 1
      AND e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2)
  ),

  -- CTE 6: Fréquence TVA collectée (ventes)
  collected_rate_freq AS (
    SELECT
      ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2) AS vat_rate,
      COUNT(*) AS cnt,
      ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) AS pct_of_total
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_accounting_entry_enriched e
    WHERE e.sp_folder_id = folder_id
      AND e.param_acc_pl_income_revenue = 1
      AND e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2)
  ),

  -- CTE 7: Écritures achats — flag anomalie
  anomalous_achats AS (
    SELECT
      e.sp_accounting_entry_id,
      e.sp_accounting_scheme_id,
      e.document_type_code,
      e.document_type_label,
      'Achat' AS operation_type,
      ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2) AS vat_rate,
      CASE
        WHEN ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2)
             NOT IN (SELECT legal_rate FROM legal_vat_rates)
        THEN 'Taux non conforme (hors référentiel légal)'
        ELSE 'Taux conforme'
      END AS anomaly_type,
      CASE
        WHEN ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2)
             NOT IN (SELECT legal_rate FROM legal_vat_rates)
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
      END AS is_anomalous,
      drf.cnt AS rate_frequency_in_folder,
      drf.pct_of_total AS rate_pct_of_folder,
      e.total_debit,
      e.total_credit,
      e.total_balance
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_accounting_entry_enriched e
    LEFT JOIN deductible_rate_freq drf
      ON ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2) = drf.vat_rate
    WHERE e.sp_folder_id = folder_id
      AND e.param_acc_pl_expense_purchase = 1
      AND e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
      AND e.document_type_code IS NOT NULL
      AND e.document_type_label IS NOT NULL
  ),

  -- CTE 8: Écritures ventes — flag anomalie
  anomalous_ventes AS (
    SELECT
      e.sp_accounting_entry_id,
      e.sp_accounting_scheme_id,
      e.document_type_code,
      e.document_type_label,
      'Vente' AS operation_type,
      ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2) AS vat_rate,
      CASE
        WHEN ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2)
             NOT IN (SELECT legal_rate FROM legal_vat_rates)
        THEN 'Taux non conforme (hors référentiel légal)'
        ELSE 'Taux conforme'
      END AS anomaly_type,
      CASE
        WHEN ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2)
             NOT IN (SELECT legal_rate FROM legal_vat_rates)
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
      END AS is_anomalous,
      crf.cnt AS rate_frequency_in_folder,
      crf.pct_of_total AS rate_pct_of_folder,
      e.total_debit,
      e.total_credit,
      e.total_balance
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_accounting_entry_enriched e
    LEFT JOIN collected_rate_freq crf
      ON ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2) = crf.vat_rate
    WHERE e.sp_folder_id = folder_id
      AND e.param_acc_pl_income_revenue = 1
      AND e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
      AND e.document_type_code IS NOT NULL
      AND e.document_type_label IS NOT NULL
  )

  -- Résultat : uniquement les écritures non conformes
  SELECT
    sp_accounting_entry_id, sp_accounting_scheme_id,
    document_type_code, document_type_label,
    operation_type, vat_rate, anomaly_type, is_anomalous,
    rate_frequency_in_folder, rate_pct_of_folder,
    total_debit, total_credit, total_balance
  FROM anomalous_achats WHERE is_anomalous = 1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
    sp_accounting_entry_id, sp_accounting_scheme_id,
    document_type_code, document_type_label,
    operation_type, vat_rate, anomaly_type, is_anomalous,
    rate_frequency_in_folder, rate_pct_of_folder,
    total_debit, total_credit, total_balance
  FROM anomalous_ventes WHERE is_anomalous = 1
  ORDER BY operation_type, document_type_code, vat_rate, sp_accounting_entry_id
functions.description: Détecte les écritures comptables achats et ventes dont le taux de TVA ne figure pas dans le référentiel légal. Priorité : taux fournis par utilisateur > référentiel pays via mv_geo_parameter_default_values_by_country. Retourne uniquement les écritures anomaliques.

functions.name: fn_vat_rate_distribution
functions.definition:
WITH
  folder_info AS (
    SELECT f.id AS sp_folder_id, f.geo_file_version_id
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_folder f
    WHERE f.id = folder_id
  ),

  legal_rates AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
      CAST(ref.param_default_value AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS legal_rate
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_geo_parameter_default_values_by_country ref
    INNER JOIN folder_info fi ON ref.geo_file_version_id = fi.geo_file_version_id
    WHERE ref.geo_parameter_type_code = 'param_general_vat_rates'
      AND (ref.country_key IS NULL OR ref.country_key = country_key)
  ),

  deductible_dist AS (
    SELECT
      'Achat' AS operation_type,
      ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2) AS vat_rate,
      COUNT(*) AS nb_entries,
      ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) AS pct_of_total,
      CASE
        WHEN ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2) IN (SELECT legal_rate FROM legal_rates)
        THEN 'Taux légal (référentiel pays)'
        ELSE 'Taux hors référentiel'
      END AS statut_referentiel
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_accounting_entry_enriched e
    WHERE e.sp_folder_id = folder_id
      AND e.param_acc_pl_expense_purchase = 1
      AND e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY
      ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2),
      CASE
        WHEN ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2) IN (SELECT legal_rate FROM legal_rates)
        THEN 'Taux légal (référentiel pays)'
        ELSE 'Taux hors référentiel'
      END
  ),

  collected_dist AS (
    SELECT
      'Vente' AS operation_type,
      ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2) AS vat_rate,
      COUNT(*) AS nb_entries,
      ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) AS pct_of_total,
      CASE
        WHEN ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2) IN (SELECT legal_rate FROM legal_rates)
        THEN 'Taux légal (référentiel pays)'
        ELSE 'Taux hors référentiel'
      END AS statut_referentiel
    FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_accounting_entry_enriched e
    WHERE e.sp_folder_id = folder_id
      AND e.param_acc_pl_income_revenue = 1
      AND e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY
      ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2),
      CASE
        WHEN ROUND(e.geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate, 2) IN (SELECT legal_rate FROM legal_rates)
        THEN 'Taux légal (référentiel pays)'
        ELSE 'Taux hors référentiel'
      END
  )

  SELECT operation_type, vat_rate, nb_entries, pct_of_total, statut_referentiel
  FROM deductible_dist
  UNION ALL
  SELECT operation_type, vat_rate, nb_entries, pct_of_total, statut_referentiel
  FROM collected_dist
  ORDER BY operation_type, nb_entries DESC
functions.description: Distribution descriptive des taux de TVA appliqués aux écritures achats et ventes. Croise les taux observés avec le référentiel légal pays sans opérationnaliser la notion anomalie. Conforme Section 4 du prompt Financial Data Analyst.

functions.name: fn_vendor_typology
functions.definition:
WITH
params AS (
  SELECT
    CAST(p_start_date AS DATE) AS start_date,
    CAST(p_end_date AS DATE) AS end_date,
    CAST(last_days AS INT) AS last_days_int
),
flagged AS (
  SELECT
    aux_account_number,
    aux_account_label,
    sp_folder_id,
    sp_accounting_entry_id,
    amount,
    accounting_entry_date,
    CASE
      WHEN accounting_entry_date >= date_add(DAY, -params.last_days_int, params.end_date) THEN ABS(amount)
      ELSE CAST(0 AS DECIMAL(30,2))
    END AS recent_amount
  FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_gl_core
  CROSS JOIN params
  WHERE sp_folder_id = p_sp_folder_id
    AND param_acc_bs_suppliers = 1
    AND aux_account_number IS NOT NULL
    AND accounting_entry_date BETWEEN params.start_date AND params.end_date
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    aux_account_number,
    aux_account_label,
    sp_folder_id,
    COUNT(*) AS nb_entries,
    COUNT(DISTINCT sp_accounting_entry_id) AS nb_accounting_entries,
    SUM(ABS(amount)) AS volume,
    SUM(recent_amount) AS recent_volume,
    MAX(accounting_entry_date) AS last_activity_date,
    SUM(amount) AS balance
  FROM flagged
  GROUP BY aux_account_number, aux_account_label, sp_folder_id
)
SELECT
  aux_account_number,
  aux_account_label,
  aggregated.sp_folder_id,
  nb_entries,
  nb_accounting_entries,
  volume,
  recent_volume,
  months_between(params.end_date, last_activity_date) AS last_activity_months,
  balance,
  SUM(volume) OVER () AS type_total_volume,
  last_activity_date
FROM aggregated
CROSS JOIN params
functions.description: For each supplier (vendor) account, returns: number of entries, number of accounting entries, total transaction volume, recent transaction volume (last N days), months since last activity, last activity date, account balance, and total volume for the account type. Used to identify inactive suppliers, suppliers with open balances, and concentration risks. One row per supplier account.

functions.name: get_avg_dso_days_for_third_parties
functions.definition:
WITH date_bounds AS (
  SELECT MAX(doc_date) AS max_doc_date
  FROM jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_gl_core
  WHERE sp_folder_id = p_sp_folder_id
    AND param_acc_bs_customers = 1
    AND doc_date IS NOT NULL
),
customer_dso AS (
  SELECT
    g.aux_account_number,
    g.aux_account_label,
    DATEDIFF(COALESCE(g.clearing_date, b.max_doc_date), g.doc_date) AS dso_days
  FROM
    jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_gl_core g
    CROSS JOIN date_bounds b
  WHERE
    g.sp_folder_id = p_sp_folder_id
    AND g.param_acc_bs_customers = 1
    AND g.doc_date IS NOT NULL
    AND g.aux_account_number IS NOT NULL
    AND g.aux_account_label IS NOT NULL
    AND g.doc_date <= b.max_doc_date
    AND (
      g.doc_date >= date_add(MONTH, -CAST(p_months AS INT), b.max_doc_date)
      OR (
        g.clearing_date >= date_add(MONTH, -CAST(p_months AS INT), b.max_doc_date)
        AND g.clearing_date <= b.max_doc_date
      )
      OR (g.clearing_date IS NULL AND g.doc_date >= date_add(MONTH, -CAST(p_months AS INT), b.max_doc_date))
    )
)
SELECT
  aux_account_number,
  aux_account_label,
  ROUND(AVG(dso_days), 2) AS avg_dso_days
FROM
  customer_dso
GROUP BY
  aux_account_number,
  aux_account_label
ORDER BY
  avg_dso_days DESC
functions.description: Returns the average DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) by customer account for a given folder and period

functions.name: get_tva_rates_applied_for_customers_by_folder_id
functions.definition:
WITH entries_dedup AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    `sp_accounting_entry_id`,
    `geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate`
  FROM
    `jxpeehqyifuv`.`geoficiency`.`mv_accounting_entry_enriched`
  WHERE
    `sp_folder_id` = p_sp_folder_id
    AND `scheme_param_acc_bs_customers` = 1
)
SELECT DISTINCT
  ROUND(`geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate`, 2) AS `taux_tva_client`
FROM
  entries_dedup
WHERE
  `geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate` IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  `taux_tva_client` ASC
functions.description: Returns the distinct deductible and collected VAT (TVA) rates found in accounting entries for the specified folder ID and customers.

functions.name: get_tva_rates_applied_for_suppliers_by_folder_id
functions.definition:
WITH entries_dedup AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    sp_accounting_entry_id,
    geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate
  FROM
    jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_accounting_entry_enriched
  WHERE
    sp_folder_id = p_sp_folder_id
    AND scheme_param_acc_bs_suppliers = 1
)
SELECT DISTINCT
  ROUND(geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate, 2) AS taux_tva_fournisseur
FROM
  entries_dedup
WHERE
  geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  taux_tva_fournisseur ASC
functions.description: Returns the distinct deductible and collected VAT (TVA) rates found in accounting entries for the specified folder ID and suppliers.

functions.name: get_tva_rates_by_folder_id
functions.definition:
WITH entries_dedup AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    sp_accounting_entry_id,
    geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate,
    geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate
  FROM
    jxpeehqyifuv.geoficiency.mv_accounting_entry_enriched
  WHERE
    sp_folder_id = p_sp_folder_id
)
SELECT
  geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate AS tva_rate
FROM
  entries_dedup
WHERE
  geo_calc_entry_deductible_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
UNION
SELECT
  geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate AS tva_rate
FROM
  entries_dedup
WHERE
  geo_calc_entry_collected_vat_rate IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  tva_rate ASC
functions.description: Returns the distinct deductible and collected VAT (TVA) rates found in accounting entries for the specified folder ID.
